design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore levels, collect assigned items, scare designated targets instructed by the Gromble, progress through environments from sewer to human world above
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing character-switching puzzle-solving with action combat; balancing exploration for collectibles against platform navigation challenges
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as young monsters attending Monster Academy, using unique abilities to navigate the human world, solve puzzles collaboratively, and complete mischievous assignments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Three-character system with mandatory switching for puzzle progression; emphasizes cooperative problem-solving (stacking, ability combination) over solo skill; licensed IP adaptation that ties gameplay to character abilities from the show

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple character abilities provide varied approaches to obstacles; Monster Manual collection gates access to 'scare' power for boss encounters

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible-based: garbage sacks and fish bones provide temporary ammo upgrades (triple trash); Monster Manuals enable scare ability for boss damage
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression tied to item collection and target completion; reaching final boss requires collecting all items to 'pass the exam'
replayability_hooks: Three playable characters with distinct abilities encourage replaying with different character loadouts
Item collection completion tied to exam passing creates completionist incentive
Multiple target locations per level suggest hidden/optional scare assignments

world_building_techniques: Monster Academy framing device (midterm exam structure)
Progressive environmental traversal from sewer to human world
NPC interaction through target scare assignments

boss_design.philosophy: Repeated encounters with Simon the Monster Hunter as final antagonist; boss health scaled by Monster Manual collection (scare power effectiveness)
notable_bosses: Simon the Monster Hunter (final boss, primary antagonist across franchise)

faction_indicators: Monster Academy hierarchy (Gromble as authority figure)
danger_signals: Simon the Monster Hunter hunts monsters
Human world portrayed as domain to navigate carefully
Exam failure implicit threat to academy progression
environmental_storytelling: Sewer starting point establishes monster habitat
Vertical progression from underground to human world above establishes hierarchy
Human locations (homes, streets) populated with targets requiring careful approach
Trash as weapon reflects monster culture and refuse ecology
